{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01B44tVrQnJw4gJg2RonGFXs","type":"tool_result","content":"200\t    def __init__(\n201\t        self,\n202\t        hidden_size: int,\n203\t        layer_id: int,\n204\t        config: Gemma4TextConfig,\n205\t        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,\n206\t        prefix: str = \"\",\n207\t    ) -> None:\n208\t        super().__init__()\n209\t        self.layer_id = layer_id\n210\t        self.hidden_size = hidden_size\n211\t        self.num_experts = config.num_experts\n212\t        self.tp_size = get_parallel().tp_size\n213\t\n214\t        # Per-expert output scale folded into routing weights so that\n215\t        # MoE's fused kernel computes: Σ_e (expert_e * w_e * scale_e)\n216\t        self.per_expert_scale = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(config.num_experts))\n217\t\n218\t        # Capture param directly to avoid closing over self in the routing closure.\n219\t        per_expert_scale = self.per_expert_scale\n220\t\n221\t        def routing_function(\n222\t            hidden_states: torch.Tensor,\n223\t            gating_output: torch.Tensor,\n224\t            topk: int,\n225\t            renormalize: bool,  # always True for Gemma4; softmax identity only holds when renormalizing\n226\t        ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n227\t            # softmax(all)[topk] / sum(softmax(all)[topk]) = softmax(topk_logits),\n228\t            # so we softmax only the top-k logits (fewer kernel launches).\n229\t            if (\n230\t                gating_output.is_cuda\n231\t                and gating_output.dim() == 2\n232\t                and gating_output.dtype\n233\t                in (torch.float16, torch.bfloat16, torch.float32)\n234\t            ):\n235\t                return gemma4_fused_routing(gating_output, per_expert_scale, topk)\n236\t\n237\t            topk_logits, topk_ids = torch.topk(gating_output, k=topk, dim=-1)\n238\t\n239\t            # Fused: softmax + per_expert_scale gather + mul + casts in one kernel\n240\t            if topk_logits.is_cuda or topk_logits.is_xpu:\n241\t                return gemma_routing_post_topk(topk_logits, topk_ids, per_expert_scale)\n242\t\n243\t            topk_weights = torch.nn.functional.softmax(topk_logits, dim=-1)\n244\t            topk_weights = topk_weights * per_expert_scale[topk_ids].to(\n245\t                topk_weights.dtype\n246\t            )\n247\t            return topk_weights.to(torch.float32), topk_ids.to(torch.int32)\n248\t\n249\t        self.topk = TopK(\n250\t            top_k=config.top_k_experts,\n251\t            layer_id=layer_id,\n252\t            custom_routing_function=routing_function,\n253\t        )\n254\t\n255\t        experts_type = get_moe_impl_class(quant_config)\n256\t\n257\t        self.experts = experts_type(\n258\t            num_experts=config.num_experts\n259\t            + get_global_server_args().ep_num_redundant_experts,\n260\t            hidden_size=config.hidden_size,\n261\t            intermediate_size=config.moe_intermediate_size,\n262\t            layer_id=layer_id,\n263\t            top_k=config.top_k_experts,\n264\t            quant_config=quant_config,\n265\t            prefix=add_prefix(\"experts\", prefix),\n266\t    def __init__(\n        self,\n        hidden_size: int,\n        layer_id: int,\n        config: Gemma4TextConfig,\n        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,\n        prefix: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.layer_id = layer_id\n        self.hidden_size = hidden_size\n        self.num_experts = config.num_experts\n        self.tp_size = get_parallel().tp_size\n\n        # Per-expert output scale folded into routing weights so that\n        # MoE's fused kernel computes: Σ_e (expert_e * w_e * scale_e)\n        self.per_expert_scale = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(config.num_experts))\n\n        # Capture param directly to avoid closing over self in the routing closure.\n        per_expert_scale = self.per_expert_scale\n\n        def routing_function(\n            hidden_states: torch.Tensor,\n            gating_output: torch.Tensor,\n            topk: int,\n            renormalize: bool,  # always True for Gemma4; softmax identity only holds when renormalizing\n        ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n            # softmax(all)[topk] / sum(softmax(all)[topk]) = softmax(topk_logits),\n            # so we softmax only the top-k logits (fewer kernel launches).\n            if (\n                gating_output.is_cuda\n                and gating_output.dim() == 2\n                and gating_output.dtype\n                in (torch.float16, torch.bfloat16, torch.float32)\n            ):\n                return gemma4_fused_routing(gating_output, per_expert_scale, topk)\n\n            topk_logits, topk_ids = torch.topk(gating_output, k=topk, dim=-1)\n\n            # Fused: softmax + per_expert_scale gather + mul + casts in one kernel\n            if topk_logits.is_cuda or topk_logits.is_xpu:\n                return gemma_routing_post_topk(topk_logits, topk_ids, per_expert_scale)\n\n            topk_weights = torch.nn.functional.softmax(topk_logits, dim=-1)\n            topk_weights = topk_weights * {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01DRdTQmUFVHW4c5kVVBBqXL","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"Per-forward-call control context.\n2\t\n3\tOwns ForwardContext — a frozen dataclass holding control configs the model\n4\tlayer reads at depth via get_forward_context(). The only mandatory field\n5\ttoday is attn_backend; pool refs are derived from attn_backend.*\n6\t(every backend caches them at __init__), so a published ForwardContext\n7\tis enough to resolve the active pools without a separate global.\n8\t\n9\tModelRunner._forward_raw publishes a fresh ForwardContext for the\n10\tduration of each forward; callers that need a per-call override (PDmux\n11\tper-stream backend, frozen-KV MTP draft loop, TBO per-child dispatch) use\n12\tdataclasses.replace and wrap the override scope with forward_context().\n13\t\n14\tDistinct from sglang.srt.model_executor.runner_backend_utils.tc_piecewise_cuda_graph.TcPiecewiseForwardContext,\n15\twhich collects compilation-time refs for the piecewise CUDA graph backend.\n16\t\n17\tConcurrency: _current is a plain module-level global, not thread-local.\n18\tThis matches the global_server_args precedent and is safe because each\n19\tforward runs synchronously on a single Python thread per worker process. If\n20\tworker threads ever share a process, migrate to contextvars.ContextVar.\n21\t\"\"\"\n22\t\n23\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n24\t\n25\tfrom contextlib import contextmanager\n26\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n27\tfrom typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Optional\n28\t\n29\tif TYPE_CHECKING:\n30\t    from sglang.srt.layers.attention.base_attn_backend import AttentionBackend\n31\t    from sglang.srt.mem_cache.memory_pool import KVCache, ReqToTokenPool\n32\t\n33\t\n34\t@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)\n35\tclass ForwardContext:\n36\t    \"\"\"Per-forward-call control configs. Read via get_forward_context();\n37\t    extend by adding fields here. The only mandatory field\ntoday is attn_backend; pool refs are derived from attn_backend.*\n(every backend caches them at __init__), so a published ForwardContext\nis enough to resolve the active pools without a separate global.\n\nModelRunner._forward_raw publishes a fresh ForwardContext for the\nduration of each forward; callers that need a per-call override (PDmux\nper-stream backend, frozen-KV MTP draft loop, TBO per-child dispatch) use\ndataclasses.replace and wrap the override scope with forward_context().\n\nDistinct from sglang.srt.model_executor.runner_backend_utils.tc_piecewise_cuda_graph.TcPiecewiseForwardContext,\nwhich collects compilation-time refs for the piecewise CUDA graph backend.\n\nConcurrency: _current is a plain module-level global, not thread-local.\nThis matches the global_server_args precedent and is safe because each\nforward runs synchronously on a single Python thread per worker process.   Out_ptr,\n    stride_x,\n    stride_r,\n    stride_o,\n    N,\n    eps,\n    HAS_SCALAR: tl.constexpr,\n    BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    \"\"\"Fused kernel: out = rmsnorm(x, w) + residual [* scalar]\n\n    When HAS_SCALAR is True, also multiplies by a scalar loaded from Scalar_ptr.\n    \"\"\"\n    row = tl.program_id(0)\n    cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)\n    mask = cols < N\n\n    x = tl.load(X_ptr + row * stride_x + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    w = tl.load(W_ptr + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    r = tl.load(Residual_ptr + row * stride_r + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(\n        tl.float32\n    )\n\n    var = tl.sum(x * x, axis=0) / N\n    rrms = tl.rsqrt(var + eps)\n    out = x * rrms * w + r\n\n    if HAS_SCALAR:\n        scalar = tl.load(Scalar_ptr).to(tl.float32)\n        out = out * scalar\n\n    tl.store(Out_ptr + row * stride_o + cols, out.to(x.dtype), mask=mask)\n\n\ndef gemma_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n    x: torch.Tensor,\n    weight: torch.Tensor,\n    residual: torch.Tensor,\n    scalar: torch.Tensor,\n    eps: float = 1e-6,\n) -> torch.Tensor:\n    \"\"\"Fused (rmsnorm(x) + residual) * scalar.\"\"\"\n    assert x.dim() == 2 and x.stride(-1) == 1, \"Expected contiguous 2D input\"\n    M, N = x.shape\n    BLOCK_SIZE = triton.next_power_of_2(N)\n    out = torch.empty_like(x)\n\n W2_ptr,\n    W3_ptr,\n    Residual_ptr,\n    Scalar_ptr,\n    Out_ptr,\n    stride_x1,\n    stride_x2,\n    stride_r,\n    stride_o,\n    N,\n    eps1,\n    eps2,\n    eps3,\n    BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    \"\"\"Fused: out = (rmsnorm(rmsnorm(x1,w1) + rmsnorm(x2,w2), w3) + residual) * scalar\"\"\"\n    row = tl.program_id(0)\n    cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)\n    mask = cols < N\n\n    x1 = tl.load(X1_ptr + row * stride_x1 + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    w1 = tl.load(W1_ptr + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    x2 = tl.load(X2_ptr + row * stride_x2 + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    w2 = tl.load(W2_ptr + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    w3 = tl.load(W3_ptr + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    r = tl.load(Residual_ptr + row * stride_r + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(\n        tl.float32\n    )\n\n    var1 = tl.sum(x1 * x1, axis=0) / N\n    norm1 = x1 * tl.rsqrt(var1 + eps1) * w1\n\n    var2 = tl.sum(x2 * x2, axis=0) / N\n    norm2 = x2 * tl.rsqrt(var2 + eps2) * w2\n\n    combined = norm1 + norm2\n\n    var3 = tl.sum(combined * combined, axis=0) / N\n    norm3 = combined * tl.rsqrt(var3 + eps3) * w3\n\n    scalar = tl.load(Scalar_ptr).to(tl.float32)\n    out = (norm3 + r) * scalar\n\n    tl.store(Out_ptr + row * stride_o + cols, out.to(x1.dtype), The kernel uses the actual\n263\t    `stride(0)` so no `.contiguous()` copy is required. Within a token, the\n264\t    last dim must be contiguous so heads pack as `h * head_dim` offsets.\n265\t\n266\t    If k and v are both None (KV-shared layer), only Q is normalized.\n267\t    \"\"\"\n268\t    assert q.is_cuda or q.is_xpu\n269\t    assert q.stride(-1) == 1, \"Q's last dim must be contiguous\"\n270\t    assert q_weight.shape[-1] == head_dim\n271\t    M = q.shape[0] if tl.constexpr,\n438\t    BLOCK_E: tl.constexpr,\n439\t):\n440\t    pid = tl.program_id(0)\n441\t    offs_e = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_E)\n442\t    valid = offs_e < E\n443\t\n444\t    logits = tl.load(\n445\t        gating_ptr + pid * stride_g_t + offs_e,\n446\t        mask=valid,\n447\t        other=-float(\"inf\"),\n448\t    ).to(tl.float32)\n449\t\n450\t    # Pack (sort_key, expert_id) into one int64 so a single signed-ascending\n451\t    # tl.sort yields logits in descending float order. The key bijection is\n452\t    # anti-monotone on the float value, and the <<32 shift moves its high bit\n453\t    # into the int64 sign bit. Ties break by expert id ascending. Invalid\n454\t    # lanes use a max key so they sort last.\n455\t    MIN32 = -2147483648\n456\t    logit_bits = logits.to(tl.int32, bitcast=True)\n457\t    sign = logit_bits >> 31\n458\t    key = tl.where(sign == 0, logit_bits ^ -1, logit_bits ^ MIN32)\n459\t    key = tl.where(valid, key, 0x7FFFFFFF)\n460\t    sk64 = key.to(tl.int64) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF\n461\t    packed = (sk64 << 32) | offs_e.to(tl.int64)\n462\t\n463\t    sorted_p = tl.sort(packed, descending=False)\n464\t    all_keys = ((sorted_p >> 32) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n465\t    all_ids = (sorted_p & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n466\t\n467\t    # Invert the key bijection to recover the original logit value.\n468\t    sign_k = all_keys >> 31\n469\t    all_bits = tl.where(sign_k < 0, all_keys ^ -1, all_keys ^ MIN32)\n470\t    all_logits = all_bits.to(tl.float32, bitcast=True)\n471\t\n472\t    # softmax over the top-K logits; max sits at index 0 (sorted descending).\n473\t    top_mask = offs_e < K\n474\t    max_l = tl.max(tl.where(top_mask, all_logits, -float(\"inf\")), axis=0)\n475\t    raw_exp = tl.where(top_mask, tl.exp(all_logits - max_l), 0.0)\n476\t\n477\t    denom = tl.sum(raw_exp, axis=0)\n478\t    denom = tl.where(denom > 0.0, denom, 1.0)\n479\t    weights = raw_exp / denom\n480\t\n481\t    scales = tl.load(\n482\t        per_expert_scale_ptr + all_ids.to(tl.int64),\n483\t        mask=top_mask,\n484\t        other=1.0,\n485\t    ).to(tl.float32)\n486\t    weights = weights * scales\n487\t\n488\t    base_off = pid * K + offs_e\n489\t SGLang TopK contract).\n509\t    \"\"\"\n510\t    assert gating_output.dim() == 2, \"expected [T, E] router logits\"\n511\t    assert per_expert_scale.dim() == 1\n512\t    assert per_expert_scale.shape[0] == gating_output.shape[1]\n513\t    T, E = gating_output.shape\n514\t    assert topk <= E, f\"topk ({topk}) must be <= E ({E})\"\n515\t    assert E <= 1024, f\"gemma4_fused_routing only supports E<=1024, got E={E}\"\n516\t\n517\t    gating_output = gating_output.contiguous()\n518\t    per_expert_scale = per_expert_scale.contiguous()\n519\t\n520\t    BLOCK_E = triton.next_power_of_2(E)\n521\t    topk_weights = torch.empty(\n522\t        (T, topk), dtype=torch.float32, device=gating_output.device\n523\t    )\n524\t    topk_ids = torch.empty((T, topk), dtype=torch.int32, device=gating_output.device)\n525\t\n526\t    if T == 0:\n527\t        return topk_weights, topk_ids\n528\t\n529\t    _gemma4_routing_kernel[(T,)](\n530\t in tl.static_range(NUM_KV_HEADS):\n                _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                    V_ptr,\n                    Q_w_ptr,\n                    stride_v_m,\n                    m,\n                    h,\n                    cols,\n                    mask,\n                    HEAD_DIM,\n                    eps,\n                    False,\n                )\n\n\ndef gemma_qkv_rmsnorm(\n    q: torch.Tensor,\n    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n    v: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n    q_weight: torch.Tensor,\n    k_weight: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n    num_q_heads: int,\n    num_kv_heads: int,\n    head_dim: int,\n    eps: float = 1e-6,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"In-place fused RMSNorm on Q, K, V for Gemma4 attention.\n\n    All three norms compute `x * rsqrt(mean(x^2) + eps)` independently per head.\n    Q is scaled by `q_weight`, K by `k_weight`, V by 1 (Gemma4's V-norm has\n    `with_scale=False`).\n\n    Inputs may be 2D `(M, num_heads * head_dim)` or strided views of a larger\n    buffer (such as q/k/v slices from `qkv.split`). The kernel uses the actual\n    `stride(0)` so no `.contiguous()` copy is required. Within a token, the\n    last dim must be contiguous so heads pack as `h * head_dim` offsets.\n\n    If k and v are both None (KV-shared layer), only Q is normalized.\n    \"\"\"\n    assert q.is_cuda or q.is_xpu\n    assert q.stride(-1) == 1, \"Q's last dim must be contiguous\"\n    assert q_weight.shape[-1] == head_dim\n    M = q.shape[0] if q.dim() >= 2 else 1\n    BLOCK = triton.next_power_of_2(head_dim)\n\n    has_kv = k is not None and v is not None\n    if has_kv:\n        assert (k.is_cuda and v.is_cuda) or (k.is_xpu and v.is_xpu)\n        assert k.stride(-1) == 1 and v.stride(-1) == 1\n        assert k_weight is not None and k_weight.shape[-1] == head_dim\n\n    if M <= 256:\n        total_heads = num_q_heads + (2 * num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0)\n        _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_kernel[(M, total_heads)](\n            q,\n            k if has_kv else q,\n            v if has_kv else q,\n            q_weight,\n            k_weight if has_kv else q_weight,\n            q.stride(0),\n            k.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n            v.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n            NUM_Q_HEADS=num_q_heads,\n            NUM_KV_HEADS=num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0,\n            HEAD_DIM=head_dim,\n            eps=eps,\n            HAS_KV=has_kv,\n            BY_HEAD=True,\n            BLOCK=BLOCK,\n        )\n        return\n\n    _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_kernel[(M,)](\n        q,\n        k if has_kv else q,\n        v if has_kv else q,\n        q_weight,\n        k_weight if has_kv else q_weight,\n        q.stride(0),\n        k.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n        v.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n        NUM_Q_HEADS=num_q_heads,\n        NUM_KV_HEADS=num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0,\n        HEAD_DIM=head_dim,\n        eps=eps,\n        HAS_KV=has_kv,\n        BY_HEAD=False,\n        BLOCK=BLOCK,\n    )\n\n\n@triton.jit\ndef _gemma_routing_post_topk_kernel(\n    Logits_ptr,\n    Ids_ptr,\n    Scale_ptr,\n    Out_weights_ptr,\n    Out_ids_ptr,\n    stride_l,\n    stride_ow,\n    stride_oi,\n    K: tl.constexpr,\n    BLOCK_K: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    \"\"\"Fused: softmax(topk_logits) * per_expert_scale[topk_ids] → float32 weights, int32 ids.\n\n    One program per token. K is the number of top-k experts (e.g. 8).\n    \"\"\"\n    row = tl.program_id(0)\n    cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_K)\n    mask = cols < K\n\n    logits = tl.load(\n        Logits_ptr + row * stride_l + cols, mask=mask, other=float(\"-inf\")\n    ).to(tl.float32)\n    ids_i64 = tl.load(Ids_ptr + row * stride_l + cols, mask=mask, other=0)\n\n    # Stable softmax\n    max_val = tl.max(logits, axis=0)\n    exp_val = tl.exp(logits - max_val)\n    sum_exp = tl.sum(exp_val, axis=0)\n    weights = exp_val / sum_exp\n\n    # Gather per_expert_scale and multiply\n    scale = tl.load(Scale_ptr + ids_i64, mask=mask, other=1.0).to(tl.float32)\n    weights = weights * scale\n\n    tl.store(Out_weights_ptr + row * stride_ow + cols, weights, mask=mask)\n    tl.store(Out_ids_ptr + row * stride_oi + cols, ids_i64.to(tl.int32), mask=mask)\n\n\ndef gemma_routing_post_topk(\n    topk_logits: torch.Tensor,\n    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,\n    per_expert_scale: torch.Tensor,\n) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Fused softmax + scale-gather + casts for Gemma4 routing.\n\n    Replaces: softmax(topk_logits) * per_expert_scale[topk_ids] → (f32, i32).\n    \"\"\"\n    B, K = topk_logits.shape\n    BLOCK_K = triton.next_power_of_2(K)\n    out_weights = torch.empty((B, K), dtype=torch.float32, device=topk_logits.device)\n    out_ids = torch.empty((B, K), dtype=torch.int32, device=topk_logits.device)\n\n    _gemma_routing_post_topk_kernel[(B,)](\n        topk_logits,\n        topk_ids,\n        per_expert_scale,\n        out_weights,\n        out_ids,\n        topk_logits.stride(0),\n        out_weights.stride(0),\n        out_ids.stride(0),\n        K=K,\n        BLOCK_K=BLOCK_K,\n    )\n    return out_weights, out_ids\n\n\ndef gemma_dual_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n    x1: torch.Tensor,\n    weight1: torch.Tensor,\n    x2: torch.Tensor,\n    weight2: torch.Tensor,\n    weight3: torch.Tensor,\n    residual: torch.Tensor,\n    scalar: torch.Tensor,\n    eps1: float = 1e-6,\n    eps2: float = 1e-6,\n    eps3: float = 1e-6,\n) -> torch.Tensor:\n    \"\"\"Fused (rmsnorm(rmsnorm(x1,w1) + rmsnorm(x2,w2), w3) + residual) * scalar.\"\"\"\n    assert x1.dim() == 2 and x1.stride(-1) == 1\n    M, N = x1.shape\n    BLOCK_SIZE = triton.next_power_of_2(N)\n    out = torch.empty_like(x1)\n\n    _gemma_dual_rmsnorm_residual_kernel[(M,)](\n        x1,\n        weight1,\n        x2,\n        weight2,\n        weight3,\n        residual,\n        scalar,\n        out,\n        x1.stride(0),\n        x2.stride(0),\n        residual.stride(0),\n        out.stride(0),\n        N,\n        eps1,\n        eps2,\n        eps3,\n        BLOCK_SIZE=BLOCK_SIZE,\n    )\n    return out\n\n\n@triton.jit\ndef _gemma4_routing_kernel(\n    gating_ptr,  # [T, E] router logits, any float dtype\n    per_expert_scale_ptr,  # [E] per-expert scale (any float dtype)\n    topk_weights_ptr,  # [T, K] fp32 out\n    topk_ids_ptr,  # [T, K] int32 out\n    stride_g_t,  # stride of gating in the token dim\n    E: tl.constexpr,\n    K: tl.constexpr,\n    BLOCK_E: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    pid = tl.program_id(0)\n    offs_e = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_E)\n    valid = offs_e < E\n\n    logits = tl.load(\n        gating_ptr + pid * stride_g_t + offs_e,\n        mask=valid,\n        other=-float(\"inf\"),\n    ).to(tl.float32)\n\n    # Pack (sort_key, expert_id) into one int64 so a single signed-ascending\n    # tl.sort yields logits in descending float order. The key bijection is\n    # anti-monotone on the float value, and the <<32 shift moves its high bit\n    # into the int64 sign bit. Ties break by expert id ascending. Invalid\n    # lanes use a max key so they sort last.\n    MIN32 = -2147483648\n    logit_bits = logits.to(tl.int32, bitcast=True)\n    sign = logit_bits >> 31\n    key = tl.where(sign == 0, logit_bits ^ -1, logit_bits ^ MIN32)\n    key = tl.where(valid, key, 0x7FFFFFFF)\n    sk64 = key.to(tl.int64) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF\n    packed = (sk64 << 32) | offs_e.to(tl.int64)\n\n    sorted_p = tl.sort(packed, descending=False)\n    all_keys = ((sorted_p >> 32) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n    all_ids = (sorted_p & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n\n    # Invert the key bijection to recover the original logit value.\n    sign_k = all_keys >> 31\n    all_bits = tl.where(sign_k < 0, all_keys ^ -1, all_keys ^ MIN32)\n    all_logits = all_bits.to(tl.float32, bitcast=True)\n\n    # softmax over the top-K logits; max sits at index 0 (sorted descending).\n    top_mask = offs_e < K\n    max_l = tl.max(tl.where(top_mask, all_logits, -float(\"inf\")), axis=0)\n    raw_exp = tl.where(top_mask, tl.exp(all_logits - max_l), 0.0)\n\n    denom = tl.sum(raw_exp, axis=0)\n    denom = tl.where(denom > 0.0, denom, 1.0)\n    weights = raw_exp / denom\n\n    scales = tl.load(\n        per_expert_scale_ptr + all_ids.to(tl.int64),\n        mask=top_mask,\n        other=1.0,\n    ).to(tl.float32)\n    weights = weights * scales\n\n    base_off = pid * K + offs_e\n    tl.store(topk_weights_ptr + base_off, weights, mask=top_mask)\n    tl.store(topk_ids_ptr + base_off, all_ids, mask=top_mask)\n\n\ndef gemma4_fused_routing(\n    gating_output: torch.Tensor,\n    per_expert_scale: torch.Tensor,\n    topk: int,\n) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"One-launch Gemma4 router.\n\n    Args:\n        gating_output: [T, E] router logits in any floating dtype; will be\n            cast to fp32 inside the kernel.\n        per_expert_scale: [E] per-expert scale, any floating dtype.\n        topk: number of experts to keep per token.\n\n    Returns:\n        topk_weights: [T, topk] fp32 (matches SGLang TopK contract).\n        topk_ids: [T, topk] int32 (matches SGLang TopK contract).\n    \"\"\"\n    assert gating_output.dim() == 2, \"expected [T, E] router logits\"\n    assert per_expert_scale.dim() == 1\n    assert per_expert_scale.shape[0] == gating_output.shape[1]\n    T, E = gating_output.shape\n    assert topk <= E, f\"topk ({topk}) must be <= E ({E})\"\n    assert E <= 1024, f\"gemma4_fused_routing only supports E<=1024, got E={E}\"\n\n    gating_output = gating_output.contiguous()\n    per_expert_scale = per_expert_scale.contiguous()\n\n    BLOCK_E = triton.next_power_of_2(E)\n    topk_weights = torch.empty(\n        (T, topk), dtype=torch.float32, device=gating_output.device\n    )\n    topk_ids = torch.empty((T, topk), dtype=torch.int32, device=gating_output.device)\n\n    if T == 0:\n        return topk_weights, topk_ids\n\n    _gemma4_routing_kernel[(T,)](\n        gating_output,\n        per_expert_scale,\n        topk_weights,\n        topk_ids,\n        gating_output.stride(0),\n        E,\n        topk,\n        BLOCK_E,\n        num_warps=1,\n    )\n    return topk_weights, topk_ids\n","numLines":342,"startLine":200,"totalLines":541}}}

Fall back to triton backend.\n--\n            backend, platform = \"triton\", \"other platforms\"\n        logger.warning(\n            f\"Use {backend} as attention backend on {platform} for Llama4 model\"\n        )\n        overrides[\"attention_backend\"] = backend\n    if is_sm100_supported() and server_args.moe_runner_backend == \"auto\":\n        if server_args.quantization in {\"fp8\", \"modelopt_fp8\"}:\n            overrides[\"moe_runner_backend\"] = \"flashinfer_trtllm\"\n            logger.info(\n                \"Use flashinfer_trtllm as MoE runner backend on SM100 for Llama4\"\n            )\n    return overrides\n\n\n@_register_for(\n    \"Gemma4ForConditionalGeneration\",\n    \"Gemma4ForCausalLM\",\n    \"Gemma4UnifiedForConditionalGeneration\",\n)\ndef _gemma4_overrides(server_args: Any, hf_config: Any) -> dict:\n    overrides: Dict[str, Any] = {}\n    default_attention_backend = \"trtllm_mha\" if is_sm100_supported() else \"triton\"\n    if server_args.is_attention_backend_not_set():\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Use {default_attention_backend} as default attention backend for Gemma4\"\n        )\n        overrides[\"attention_backend\"] = default_attention_backend\n    # If only one split backend is set, keep the other side on a\n    # Gemma4-compatible fallback instead of letting generic backend selection\n    # choose an unsupported backend later.\n    elif server_args.attention_backend is None:\n        overrides[\"attention_backend\"] = default_attention_backend\n    if is_sm100_supported() and server_args.moe_runner_backend == \"auto\":\n        if server_args.get_model_config().quantization == \"modelopt_fp4\":\n            overrides[\"quantization\"] = \"modelopt_fp4\"\n            overrides[\"moe_runner_backend\"] = \"flashinfer_trtllm\"\n            logger.info(\n                \"Use flashinfer_trtllm as MoE runner backend on \"\n                \"SM100 for Gemma-4 (modelopt_fp4)\"\n            )\n    return overrides\n\n\n@_register_for(\"MossVLForConditionalGeneration\")\ndef _moss_vl_overrides(server_args: Any, hf_config: Any) -> dict:\n    overrides: Dict[str, Any] = {}\n    if server_args.is_attention_backend_not_set():\n        overrides[\"prefill_attention_backend\"] = \"flashinfer\"","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

==============================================================================\n\"\"\"\nMemory-efficient attention for prefill.\nIt supports page size = 1 and prefill with KV cache (i.e. extend).\n\"\"\"\n\nimport torch\nimport triton\nimport triton.language as tl\n\nfrom sglang.srt.layers.attention.triton_ops.decode_attention import _extract_kv_strides\nfrom sglang.srt.layers.attention.triton_ops.prefill_attention import (\n    context_attention_fwd,\n)\nfrom sglang.srt.utils import is_cuda, is_gfx95_supported, is_hip\n\n_is_cuda = is_cuda()\nif _is_cuda:\n    CUDA_CAPABILITY = torch.cuda.get_device_capability()\n\n_is_hip = is_hip()\n_is_gfx95 = _is_hip and is_gfx95_supported()\n\n\ndef _get_block_sizes_for_extend_attention(Lq: int, Lv: int):\n    \"\"\"\n    Get block sizes and configuration for extend attention kernels.\n\n    Args:\n        Lq: Query head dimension\n        Lv: Value head dimension\n\n    Returns:\n        tuple: (BLOCK_DMODEL, BLOCK_DPE, BLOCK_DV, BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, num_warps)\n    \"\"\"\n    # Determine BLOCK_DMODEL and BLOCK_DPE based on head dimension\n    if Lq == 576:\n        BLOCK_DMODEL = 512\n        BLOCK_DPE = 64\n    elif Lq == 288:\n        BLOCK_DMODEL = 256\n        BLOCK_DPE = 32\n    elif Lq == 192:\n        BLOCK_DMODEL = 128\n        BLOCK_DPE = 64\n    else:\n        BLOCK_DMODEL = triton.next_power_of_2(Lq)\n        BLOCK_DPE = 0\n\n    BLOCK_DV = triton.next_power_of_2(Lv)\n\n    # Determine BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, and num_warps based on hardware\n    if _is_hip:\n        if _is_gfx95 and 128 < Lq <= 256:\n            # gfx950 (CDNA4), 128 < head_dim <= 256: a larger query tile halves KV bytes\n            # streamed per call (each workgroup reads the whole prefix); 8 warps\n            # hide the loads. Measured on MI350X head_dim 256: -36% kernel time,\n            # 28% -> 44% MFU, numerically equivalent (BLOCK_N reduction order\n            # unchanged). Other AMD archs / head dims keep the default below.\n            BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N = (128, 64)\n            num_warps = 8\n        else:\n            BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N = (64, 64)\n            num_warps = 4\n    else:\n        if _is_cuda and CUDA_CAPABILITY[0] == 12:\n            # sm120 workstation Blackwell architecture (RTX Pro 6000) has a much smaller shared memory size (100K)\n            if Lq <= 128:\n                BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N = (64, 128)\n            elif Lq <= 256:\n                BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N = (64, 64)\n            else:\n                BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N = (32, 32)\n        elif _is_cuda and CUDA_CAPABILITY[0] == 10:\n            # Blackwell data-center architecture (GB200, B200, sm_100a)\n            # sm_100a has different register constraints from Hopper; Hopper block sizes\n            # cause PTX register exhaustion (>255 regs) for large head dims (Lq=512).\n            if Lq <= 256:\n                BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N = (64, 64)\n            else:\n                BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N = (16, 64)\n        elif _is_cuda and CUDA_CAPABILITY[0] >= 9:\n            # Hopper architecture (H100, etc.)\n            if Lq <= 128:\n                BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N = (128, 64)\n            elif Lq <= 256:\n                BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N = (64, 64)\n            else:","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1211}}}

Gemma4 residual pattern following JAX implementation:\n652\t        # 1. input_norm(x) -> attn -> post_attn_norm -> ADD residual\n653\t        # 2. pre_ff_norm -> mlp -> post_ff_norm -> ADD residual\n654\t        #\n655\t        # Optimization: fuse \"post_attn_norm(h) + residual; pre_ff_norm(...)\"\n656\t        # into \"post_attn_norm(h); pre_ff_norm(h, residual)\" using\n657\t        # gemma_fused_add_rmsnorm which computes:\n658\t        #   residual = h + residual (in-place)\n659\t        #   h = gemma_norm(residual)\n660\t        residual = hidden_states\n661\t\n662\t        # Apply input layernorm\n663\t        hidden_states = self.input_layernorm(hidden_states)\n664\t        hidden_states = self.self_attn(\n665\t            positions=positions,\n666\t            hidden_states=hidden_states,\n667\t            forward_batch=forward_batch,\n668\t        )\n669\t        hidden_states = self.post_attention_layernorm(hidden_states)\n670\t\n671\t        if self.enable_moe_block:\n672\t            # Fuse: hidden_states + residual -> residual; pre_ff_norm(residual) -> hidden_states\n673\t            # Also need raw (unfused) residual for router and pre_ff_norm_2\n674\t            hidden_states, residual = self.pre_feedforward_layernorm(\n675\t                hidden_states, residual\n676\t            )\n677\t            # For MoE: router and pre_ff_norm_2 need the unfused residual\n678\t            # (which is now updated to post_attn_out + old_residual)\n679\t            moe_input = residual\n680\t\n681\t            # Dense MLP branch\n682\t            hidden_states_1 = self.mlp(hidden_states)\n683\t\n684\t            # MoE branch: router sees residual (= post_attn_out + old_residual)\n685\t            router_logits = self.router(moe_input)\n686\t            hidden_states_2 = self.pre_feedforward_layernorm_2(moe_input)\n687\t            hidden_states_2 = self.moe(hidden_states_2, router_logits)\n688\t\n689\t            # Fused: (rmsnorm(rmsnorm(h1,w1) + rmsnorm(h2,w2), w3) + residual) * scalar\n690\t            if (\n691\t                not self.has_ple\n692\t                and (hidden_states_1.is_cuda or hidden_states_1.is_xpu)\n693\t                and hidden_states_1.dim() == 2\n694\t            ):\n695\t                norm1 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_1\n696\t                norm2 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_2\n697\t                norm3 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm\n698\t                hidden_states = gemma_dual_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n699\t                    hidden_states_1,\n700\t                    norm1.weight.data,\n701\t                    hidden_states_2,\n702\t                    norm2.weight.data,\n703\t                    norm3.weight.data,\n704\t                    residual,\n705\t                    self.layer_scalar,\n706\t                    norm1.variance_epsilon,\n707\t                    norm2.variance_epsilon,\n708\t                    norm3.variance_epsilon,\n709\t                )\n710\t                return hidden_states, None\n711\t\n712\t            hidden_states_1 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_1(hidden_states_1)\n713\t            hidden_states_2 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_2(hidden_states_2)\n714\t\n715\t            # Combine branches\n716\t            hidden_states = hidden_states_1 + hidden_states_2\n717\t        else:\n718\t            # Fuse: hidden_states + residual -> residual; pre_ff_norm(residual) -> hidden_states\n719\t            hidden_states, residual = self.pre_feedforward_layernorm(\n720\t                hidden_states, residual\n721\t            )\n722\t            hidden_states = self.mlp(hidden_states)\n723\t\n724\t        if (\n725\t            not self.has_ple\n726\t            and self.moe is None\n727\t            and (hidden_states.is_cuda or hidden_states.is_xpu)\n728\t            and hidden_states.dim() == 2\n729\t        ):\n730\t            # Fused: (post_ff_norm(h) + residual) * layer_scalar in one kernel\n731\t            norm = self.post_feedforward_layernorm\n732\t            hidden_states = gemma_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n733\t                hidden_states,\n734\t                norm.weight.data,\n735\t                residual,\n736\t                self.layer_scalar,\n737\t                norm.variance_epsilon,\n738\t            )\n739\t        else:\n740\t            hidden_states = self.post_feedforward_layernorm(hidden_states)\n741\t            hidden_states = hidden_states + residual\n742\t\n743\t            if self.has_ple and per_layer_input is not None:\n744\t                gate, _ = self.per_layer_input_gate(hidden_states)\n745\t                gate = torch.nn.functional.gelu(gate, approximate=\"tanh\")\n746\t                gated_per_layer = gate * per_layer_input\n747\t                per_layer_contribution, _ = self.per_layer_projection(gated_per_layer)\n748\t                per_layer_contribution = self.post_per_layer_input_norm(\n749\t                    per_layer_contribution\n750\t                )\n751\t                hidden_states = hidden_states + per_layer_contribution\n752\t\n753\t            hidden_states = hidden_states * self.layer_scalar\n754\t        return hidden_states, None\n755\t\n756\t\n757\tclass Gemma4TextModel(PreTrainedModel):\n758\t    def __init__(\n759\t        self,\n760\t        config: Gemma4TextConfig,\n761\t        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,\n762\t        prefix: str = \"\",\n763\t    ) -> None:\n764\t        super().__init__(config=config)\n765\t        self.config = config\n766\t        self.quant_config = quant_config\n767\t        self.vocab_size = config.vocab_size\n768\t        self.padding_idx = getattr(config, \"pad_token_id\", None)\n769\t        self.pp_group = get_pp_group()\n770\t\n771\t        # Token / per-layer embedding tables and the per-layer projection only\n772\t        # produce activations consumed at the model entry, so they live on the\n773\t        # first PP rank only.  Other ranks substitute PPMissingLayer so that\n774\t        # parameter iteration still works (load_weights skips them explicitly).\n775\t        self.hidden_size = config.hidden_size\n776\t        self.hidden_size_per_layer_input = (\n777\t            getattr(config, \"hidden_size_per_layer_input\", None) or 0\n778\t        )\n779\t        self.vocab_size_per_layer_input = (\n780\t            getattr(config, \"vocab_size_per_layer_input\", None) or config.vocab_size\n781\t        )\n782\t\n783\t        # PLE-enabled variants (E2B/E4B) forward `per_layer_inputs` through\n784\t        # the PP proxy, but cuda_graph_runner hardcodes the proxy schema to\n785\t        # {hidden_states, residual} and silently drops any extra keys at\n786\t        # replay time.  Empirically this corrupts E4B output to garbage on\n787\t        # non-first PP ranks (eager path produces correct output and\n788\t        # GSM8K ~0.92, cuda-graph path emits token soup).  Refuse the\n789\t        # combination until the runner becomes schema-aware; users can run\n790\t        # PP + PLE eagerly with --disable-cuda-graph.\n791\t        if self.pp_group.world_size > 1 and self.hidden_size_per_layer_input > 0:\n792\t            sa = get_global_server_args()\n793\t            if sa is not None and not sa.disable_cuda_graph:\n794\t                raise ValueError(\n795\t                    \"Pipeline parallelism is currently incompatible with \"\n796\t                    \"per-layer-input (PLE) embeddings under CUDA graph: \"\n797\t                    \"the runner's PP proxy schema is hardcoded to \"\n798\t                    \"{hidden_states, residual} and silently drops \"\n799\t                    \"per_layer_inputs, corrupting per-layer contributions on \""}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"584b7119-1513-41b0-94d2-3a81c808c71e","uuid":"a48ec87d-3024-4588-8860-1b59f6f78f74","timestamp":"2026-07-17T17:47:23.865Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/models/gemma4_causal.py","content":"                self.kv_shared_layer_index if self.is_kv_shared_layer else self.layer_id\n            ),\n            logit_cap=0.0,\n            sliding_window_size=self.sliding_window,\n            quant_config=quant_config,\n            prefix=add_prefix(\"attn\", prefix),\n        )\n\n    def forward(\n        self,\n        positions: torch.Tensor,\n        hidden_states: torch.Tensor,\n        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,\n        **kwargs,\n    ):\n        qkv, _ = self.qkv_proj(hidden_states)\n        q, k, v = qkv.split([self.q_size, self.kv_size, self.kv_size], dim=-1)\n\n        # standard norm*weight (scale_shift==0) and v_norm has weight=ones\n        # (with_scale=False) — the canonical Gemma4 attention configuration.\n        is_kv_shared = (\n            self.is_kv_shared_layer and self.kv_shared_layer_index is not None\n        )\n        can_fuse_qkv_norm = (\n            (q.is_cuda or q.is_xpu)\n            and self.q_norm.scale_shift == 0.0\n            and self.k_norm.scale_shift == 0.0\n            and not self.v_norm.with_scale\n        )\n        if can_fuse_qkv_norm:\n            if is_kv_shared:\n                gemma_qkv_rmsnorm(\n                    q,\n                    None,\n                    None,\n                    self.q_norm.weight.data,\n                    None,\n                    num_q_heads=self.num_heads,\n                    num_kv_heads=self.num_kv_heads,\n                    head_dim=self.head_dim,\n                    eps=self.q_norm.eps,\n                )\n                k = None\n                v = None\n            else:\n                gemma_qkv_rmsnorm(\n                    q,\n                    k,\n                    v,\n                    self.q_norm.weight.data,\n                    self.k_norm.weight.data,\n                    num_q_heads=self.num_heads,\n                    num_kv_heads=self.num_kv_heads,\n                    head_dim=self.head_dim,\n                    eps=self.q_norm.eps,\n                )\n                # Match the original norm path's output shapes: q stays 2D,\n                # k/v become 3D so the subsequent `.flatten(-2, -1)` works.\n                # Use reshape (not view) since k/v are strided slice views of\n                # the qkv buffer and may not satisfy view's contiguity rules.\n                k = k.reshape(-1, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_dim)\n                v = v.reshape(-1, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_dim)\n        else:\n            q = q.unflatten(-1, (self.num_heads, self.head_dim))\n            q = self.q_norm(q)\n            q = q.flatten(-2, -1)\n            if is_kv_shared:\n                k = None\n                v = None\n            else:\n                k = k.unflatten(-1, (self.num_kv_heads, self.head_dim))\n                k = self.k_norm(k)\n                v = v.unflatten(-1, (self.num_kv_heads, self.head_dim))\n                v = self.v_norm(v)\n\n        # Apply rotary embedding\n        use_fused_kv = False\n        if k is not None:\n            k = k.flatten(-2, -1)\n            # Fuse RoPE + KV-cache write for non-SWA layers with bf16 cache\n            # DISABLED: causes accuracy regression in launch_server path\n            can_fuse = False\n            if can_fuse:\n                fused_arg = create_fused_set_kv_buffer_arg(\n                    value=v.flatten(-2, -1) if v.dim() == 3 else v,\n                    layer=self.attn,\n                    forward_batch=forward_batch,\n                )\n                use_fused_kv = True\n            else:\n                fused_arg = None\n            q, k = self.rotary_emb(positions, q, k, fused_set_kv_buffer_arg=fused_arg)\n            k = k.unflatten(-1, (self.num_kv_heads, self.head_dim))\n        else:\n            # Rotary embedding requires a key input; use zeros since KV is shared from another layer\n            dummy_k = torch.zeros_like(q[:, : self.kv_size])\n            q, _ = self.rotary_emb(positions, q, dummy_k)\n\n        q = q.unflatten(-1, (self.num_heads, self.head_dim))\n        attn_output = self.attn(\n            q,\n            k,\n            v,\n            forward_batch=forward_batch,\n            save_kv_cache=not self.is_kv_shared_layer and not use_fused_kv,\n        )\n        if attn_output.dim() == 3:\n            attn_output = attn_output.flatten(-2, -1)\n        output, _ = self.o_proj(attn_output)\n\n        return output\n\n\nclass Gemma4DecoderLayer(nn.Module):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        layer_id: int,\n        config: PretrainedConfig,\n        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,\n        prefix: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.hidden_size = config.hidden_size\n        self.hidden_size_per_layer_input = (\n            getattr(config, \"hidden_size_per_layer_input\", None) or 0\n        )\n\n        self.layer_id = layer_id\n\n        # Gemma 4 uses different head dimensions for sliding vs full attention\n        layer_type = config.layer_types[layer_id]\n        self.is_full_attention = layer_type == \"full_attention\"\n        if self.is_full_attention:\n            head_dim = config.head_dim  # following sglang naming\n        else:\n            head_dim = getattr(config, \"swa_head_dim\", config.head_dim)\n\n        self.self_attn = Gemma4Attention(\n            layer_id=layer_id,\n            config=config,\n            max_position_embeddings=config.max_position_embeddings,\n            head_dim=head_dim,\n            quant_config=quant_config,\n            prefix=add_prefix(\"self_attn\", prefix),\n        )\n\n        first_kv_shared_layer_idx = config.num_hidden_layers - getattr(\n            config, \"num_kv_shared_layers\", 0\n        )\n        is_kv_shared_layer = self.layer_id >= first_kv_shared_layer_idx > 0\n        use_double_wide_mlp = (\n            getattr(config, \"use_double_wide_mlp\", False) and is_kv_shared_layer\n        )\n        layer_intermediate_size = config.intermediate_size * (\n            2 if use_double_wide_mlp else 1\n        )\n\n        self.mlp = Gemma4MLP(\n            hidden_size=self.hidden_size,\n            intermediate_size=layer_intermediate_size,\n            hidden_activation=config.hidden_activation,\n            quant_config=quant_config,\n            prefix=add_prefix(\"mlp\", prefix),\n        )\n\n        self.input_layernorm = RMSNorm(config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps)\n        self.post_attention_layernorm = RMSNorm(\n            config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n        )\n        self.pre_feedforward_layernorm = RMSNorm(\n            config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n        )\n        self.post_feedforward_layernorm = RMSNorm(\n            config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n        )\n\n        # Per-Layer Embedding (PLE) components — present in each decoder layer\n        if self.hidden_size_per_layer_input > 0:\n            # Gate: projects hidden_states → per-layer dim for gating\n            self.per_layer_input_gate = ReplicatedLinear(\n                self.hidden_size,\n                self.hidden_size_per_layer_input,\n                bias=False,\n                quant_config=quant_config,\n                prefix=add_prefix(\"per_layer_input_gate\", prefix),\n            )\n            # Projection: projects gated per-layer input back → hidden size\n            self.per_layer_projection = ReplicatedLinear(\n                self.hidden_size_per_layer_input,\n                self.hidden_size,\n                bias=False,\n                quant_config=quant_config,\n                prefix=add_prefix(\"per_layer_projection\", prefix),\n            )\n            self.post_per_layer_input_norm = Gemma4RMSNorm(\n                config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n            )\n        else:\n            self.per_layer_input_gate = None\n            self.per_layer_projection = None\n            self.post_per_layer_input_norm = None\n\n        # Parallel MoE\n        self.enable_moe_block = getattr(config, \"enable_moe_block\", False)\n        if self.enable_moe_block:\n            self.router = Gemma4Router(\n                config,\n                quant_config=quant_config,\n                prefix=add_prefix(\"router\", prefix),\n            )\n            self.moe = Gemma4MoE(\n                hidden_size=self.hidden_size,\n                layer_id=layer_id,\n                config=config,\n                quant_config=quant_config,\n                prefix=add_prefix(\"moe\", prefix),\n            )\n\n            self.post_feedforward_layernorm_1 = RMSNorm(\n                config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n            )\n            self.post_feedforward_layernorm_2 = RMSNorm(\n                config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n            )\n            self.pre_feedforward_layernorm_2 = RMSNorm(\n                config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n            )\n        else:\n            self.router = None\n            self.moe = None\n            self.post_feedforward_layernorm_1 = None\n            self.post_feedforward_layernorm_2 = None\n            self.pre_feedforward_layernorm_2 = None\n\n        self.register_buffer(\"layer_scalar\", torch.ones(1), persistent=True)\n        self.has_ple = self.hidden_size_per_layer_input > 0\n        self.prefix = prefix\n\n    def forward(\n        self,\n        positions: torch.Tensor,\n        hidden_states: torch.Tensor,\n        per_layer_input: torch.Tensor,\n        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,\n        **kwargs,\n    ) -> tuple[\n        torch.FloatTensor, Optional[tuple[torch.FloatTensor, torch.FloatTensor]]\n    ]:\n        # Gemma4 residual pattern following JAX implementation:\n        # 1. input_norm(x) -> attn -> post_attn_norm -> ADD residual\n        # 2. pre_ff_norm -> mlp -> post_ff_norm -> ADD residual\n        #\n        # Optimization: fuse \"post_attn_norm(h) + residual; pre_ff_norm(...)\"\n        # into \"post_attn_norm(h); pre_ff_norm(h, residual)\" using\n        # gemma_fused_add_rmsnorm which computes:\n        #   residual = h + residual (in-place)\n        #   h = gemma_norm(residual)\n        residual = hidden_states\n\n        # Apply input layernorm\n        hidden_states = self.input_layernorm(hidden_states)\n        hidden_states = self.self_attn(\n            positions=positions,\n            hidden_states=hidden_states,\n            forward_batch=forward_batch,\n        )\n        hidden_states = self.post_attention_layernorm(hidden_states)\n\n        if self.enable_moe_block:\n            # Fuse: hidden_states + residual -> residual; pre_ff_norm(residual) -> hidden_states\n            # Also need raw (unfused) residual for router and pre_ff_norm_2\n            hidden_states, residual = self.pre_feedforward_layernorm(\n                hidden_states, residual\n            )\n            # For MoE: router and pre_ff_norm_2 need the unfused residual\n            # (which is now updated to post_attn_out + old_residual)\n            moe_input = residual\n\n            # Dense MLP branch\n            hidden_states_1 = self.mlp(hidden_states)\n\n            # MoE branch: router sees residual (= post_attn_out + old_residual)\n            router_logits = self.router(moe_input)\n            hidden_states_2 = self.pre_feedforward_layernorm_2(moe_input)\n            hidden_states_2 = self.moe(hidden_states_2, router_logits)\n\n            # Fused: (rmsnorm(rmsnorm(h1,w1) + rmsnorm(h2,w2), w3) + residual) * scalar\n            if (\n                not self.has_ple\n                and (hidden_states_1.is_cuda or hidden_states_1.is_xpu)\n                and hidden_states_1.dim() == 2\n            ):\n                norm1 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_1\n                norm2 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_2\n                norm3 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm\n                hidden_states = gemma_dual_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n                    hidden_states_1,\n                    norm1.weight.data,\n                    hidden_states_2,\n                    norm2.weight.data,\n                    norm3.weight.data,\n                    residual,\n                    self.layer_scalar,\n                    norm1.variance_epsilon,\n                    norm2.variance_epsilon,\n                    norm3.variance_epsilon,\n                )\n                return hidden_states, None\n\n            hidden_states_1 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_1(hidden_states_1)\n            hidden_states_2 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_2(hidden_states_2)\n\n            # Combine branches\n            hidden_states = hidden_states_1 + hidden_states_2\n        else:\n            # Fuse: hidden_states + residual -> residual; pre_ff_norm(residual) -> hidden_states\n            hidden_states, residual = self.pre_feedforward_layernorm(\n                hidden_states, residual\n            )\n            hidden_states = self.mlp(hidden_states)\n\n        if (\n            not self.has_ple\n            and self.moe is None\n            and (hidden_states.is_cuda or hidden_states.is_xpu)\n            and hidden_states.dim() == 2\n        ):\n            # Fused: (post_ff_norm(h) + residual) * layer_scalar in one kernel\n            norm = self.post_feedforward_layernorm\n            hidden_states = gemma_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n                hidden_states,\n                norm.weight.data,\n                residual,\n                self.layer_scalar,\n                norm.variance_epsilon,\n            )\n        else:\n            hidden_states = self.post_feedforward_layernorm(hidden_states)\n            hidden_states = hidden_states + residual\n\n            if self.has_ple and per_layer_input is not None:\n                gate, _ = self.per_layer_input_gate(hidden_states)\n                gate = torch.nn.functional.gelu(gate, approximate=\"tanh\")\n                gated_per_layer = gate * per_layer_input\n                per_layer_contribution, _ = self.per_layer_projection(gated_per_layer)\n                per_layer_contribution = self.post_per_layer_input_norm(\n                    per_layer_contribution\n                )\n                hidden_states = hidden_states + per_layer_contribution\n\n            hidden_states = hidden_states * self.layer_scalar\n        return hidden_states, None\n\n\nclass Gemma4TextModel(PreTrainedModel):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        config: Gemma4TextConfig,\n        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,\n        prefix: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__(config=config)\n        self.config = config\n        self.quant_config = quant_config\n        self.vocab_size = config.vocab_size\n        self.padding_idx = getattr(config, \"pad_token_id\", None)\n        self.pp_group = get_pp_group()\n\n        # Token / per-layer embedding tables and the per-layer projection only\n        # produce activations consumed at the model entry, so they live on the\n        # first PP rank only.  Other ranks substitute PPMissingLayer so that\n        # parameter iteration still works (load_weights skips them explicitly).\n        self.hidden_size = config.hidden_size\n        self.hidden_size_per_layer_input = (\n            getattr(config, \"hidden_size_per_layer_input\", None) or 0\n        )\n        self.vocab_size_per_layer_input = (\n            getattr(config, \"vocab_size_per_layer_input\", None) or config.vocab_size\n        )\n\n        # PLE-enabled variants (E2B/E4B) forward `per_layer_inputs` through\n        # the PP proxy, but cuda_graph_runner hardcodes the proxy schema to\n        # {hidden_states, residual} and silently drops any extra keys at\n        # replay time.  Empirically this corrupts E4B output to garbage on\n        # non-first PP ranks (eager path produces correct output and\n        # GSM8K ~0.92, cuda-graph path emits token soup).  Refuse the\n        # combination until the runner becomes schema-aware; users can run\n        # PP + PLE eagerly with --disable-cuda-graph.\n        if self.pp_group.world_size > 1 and self.hidden_size_per_layer_input > 0:\n            sa = get_global_server_args()\n            if sa is not None and not sa.disable_cuda_graph:\n                raise ValueError(\n                    \"Pipeline parallelism is currently incompatible with \"\n                    \"per-layer-input (PLE) embeddings under CUDA graph: \"\n                    \"the runner's PP proxy schema is hardcoded to \"\n                    \"{hidden_states, residual} and silently drops \"\n                    \"per_layer_inputs, corrupting per-layer contributions on \"","numLines":400,"startLine":400,"totalLines":1422}}}

The token (M) stride is\n184\t    taken from stride_*_m so the kernel works on strided views (e.g. slices of a\n185\t    larger qkv buffer produced by `qkv.split`) without requiring `.contiguous()`\n186\t    copies. V uses `weight=ones` semantics so the multiply-by-weight is omitted.\n187\t    \"\"\"\n188\t    m = tl.program_id(0)\n189\t    cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK)\n190\t    mask = cols < HEAD_DIM\n191\t\n192\t    if BY_HEAD:\n193\t        h_all = tl.program_id(1)\n194\t        if h_all < NUM_Q_HEADS:\n195\t            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n196\t                Q_ptr, Q_w_ptr, stride_q_m, m, h_all, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n197\t            )\n198\t        elif HAS_KV and h_all < NUM_Q_HEADS + NUM_KV_HEADS:\n199\t            h = h_all - BY_HEAD=True: grid is (M, total_heads), one program per token/head.\n    - BY_HEAD=False: grid is (M,), one program per token looping over heads.\n\n    Layout assumption: each tensor's last dim packs (num_heads, head_dim)\n    contiguously so per-head offset is `h * HEAD_DIM`. The token (M) stride is\n    taken from stride_*_m so the kernel works on strided views (e.g. slices of a\n    larger qkv buffer produced by `qkv.split`) without requiring `.contiguous()`\n    copies. V uses `weight=ones` semantics so the multiply-by-weight is omitted.\n    \"\"\"\n    m = tl.program_id(0)\n    cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK)\n    mask = cols < HEAD_DIM\n\n    if BY_HEAD:\n        h_all = tl.program_id(1)\n        if h_all < NUM_Q_HEADS:\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                Q_ptr, Q_w_ptr, stride_q_m, m, h_all, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n            )\n        elif HAS_KV and h_all < NUM_Q_HEADS + NUM_KV_HEADS:\n            h = h_all - NUM_Q_HEADS\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                K_ptr, K_w_ptr, stride_k_m, m, h, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n            )\n        elif HAS_KV:\n            h = h_all - NUM_Q_HEADS - NUM_KV_HEADS\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                V_ptr, Q_w_ptr, stride_v_m, m, h, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, False\n            )\n    else:\n        for h in tl.static_range(NUM_Q_HEADS):\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                Q_ptr, Q_w_ptr, stride_q_m, m, h, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n            )\n\n        if HAS_KV:","numLines":80,"startLine":135,"totalLines":541}}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_vrtx_01HMUpG6SB2mVawAJfbN1Moj","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now let me think about concrete optimizations. Looking at the code, I can see potential improvements:\n\n1. **The gemma4_fused_routing kernel** uses a complex bitwise transformation for sorting. tl.constexpr,\n):\n    off = m * stride_m + h * HEAD_DIM + cols\n    x = tl.load(X_ptr + off, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    rrms = tl.rsqrt(tl.sum(x * x, axis=0) / HEAD_DIM + eps)\n    out mask=mask)\n\n\n@triton.jit\ndef _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_kernel(\n    Q_ptr,\n    K_ptr,\n    V_ptr,\n    Q_w_ptr,\n    K_w_ptr,\n    stride_q_m,\n    stride_k_m,\n    stride_v_m,\n    NUM_Q_HEADS: tl.constexpr,\n    NUM_KV_HEADS: tl.constexpr,\n    HEAD_DIM: tl.constexpr,\n    eps,\n    HAS_KV: tl.constexpr,\n    BY_HEAD: tl.constexpr,\n    BLOCK: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    \"\"\"Fused per-head RMSNorm for Q, K, V.\n\n    The same kernel supports two launch shapes:\n    - BY_HEAD=True: grid is (M, total_heads), one program per token/head.\n    - BY_HEAD=False: grid is (M,), one program per token looping over heads.\n\n    Layout assumption: each tensor's last dim packs (num_heads, head_dim)\n    contiguously so per-head offset is `h * HEAD_DIM`. The token (M) stride is\n    taken from stride_*_m so the kernel works on strided views (e.g. slices of a\n    larger qkv buffer produced by `qkv.split`) without requiring `.contiguous()`\n    copies. V uses `weight=ones` semantics so the multiply-by-weight is omitted.\n    \"\"\"\n    m = tl.program_id(0)\n    cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK)\n    mask = cols < HEAD_DIM\n\n    if BY_HEAD:\n        h_all = tl.program_id(1)\n        if h_all < NUM_Q_HEADS:\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                Q_ptr, Q_w_ptr, stride_q_m, m, h_all, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n            )\n        elif HAS_KV and h_all < NUM_Q_HEADS + NUM_KV_HEADS:\n            h = h_all - NUM_Q_HEADS\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                K_ptr, K_w_ptr, stride_k_m, m, h, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n            )\n        elif HAS_KV:\n            h = h_all - NUM_Q_HEADS - NUM_KV_HEADS\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                V_ptr, Q_w_ptr, stride_v_m, m, h, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, False\n            )\n    else:\n        for h in tl.static_range(NUM_Q_HEADS):\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                Q_ptr, Q_w_ptr, stride_q_m, m, h, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n            )\n\n        if HAS_KV:\n            for h in tl.static_range(NUM_KV_HEADS):\n                _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                    K_ptr,\n                    K_w_ptr,\n                    stride_k_m,\n                    m,\n                    h,\n                    cols,\n                    mask,\n                    HEAD_DIM,\n                    eps,\n                    True,\n                )\n\n            for h in tl.static_range(NUM_KV_HEADS):\n                _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                    V_ptr,\n                    Q_w_ptr,\n                    stride_v_m,\n                    m,\n                    h,\n                    cols,\n                    mask,\n                    HEAD_DIM,\n                    eps,\n                    False,\n                )\n\n\ndef gemma_qkv_rmsnorm(\n    q: torch.Tensor,\n    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n    v: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n    q_weight: torch.Tensor,\n    k_weight: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n    num_q_heads: int,\n    num_kv_heads: int,\n    head_dim: int,\n    eps: float = 1e-6,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"In-place fused RMSNorm on Q, K, V for Gemma4 attention.\n\n    All three norms compute `x * rsqrt(mean(x^2) + eps)` independently per head.\n    Q is scaled by `q_weight`, K by `k_weight`, V by 1 (Gemma4's V-norm has\n    `with_scale=False`).\n\n    Inputs may be 2D `(M, num_heads * head_dim)` or strided views of a larger\n    buffer (such as q/k/v slices from `qkv.split`). The kernel uses the actual\n    `stride(0)` so no `.contiguous()` copy is required. Within a token, the\n    last dim must be contiguous so heads pack as `h * head_dim` offsets.\n\n    If k and v are both None (KV-shared layer), only Q is normalized.\n    \"\"\"\n    assert q.is_cuda or q.is_xpu\n    assert q.stride(-1) == 1, \"Q's last dim must be contiguous\"\n    assert q_weight.shape[-1] == head_dim\n    M = q.shape[0] if q.dim() >= 2 else 1\n    BLOCK = triton.next_power_of_2(head_dim)\n\n    has_kv = k is not None and v is not None\n    if has_kv:\n        assert (k.is_cuda and v.is_cuda) or (k.is_xpu and v.is_xpu)\n        assert k.stride(-1) == 1 and v.stride(-1) == 1\n        assert k_weight is not None and k_weight.shape[-1] == head_dim\n\n    if M <= 256:\n        total_heads = num_q_heads + (2 * num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0)\n        _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_kernel[(M, total_heads)](\n            q,\n            k if has_kv else q,\n            v if has_kv else q,\n            q_weight,\n            k_weight if has_kv else q_weight,\n            q.stride(0),\n            k.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n            v.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n            NUM_Q_HEADS=num_q_heads,\n            NUM_KV_HEADS=num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0,\n            HEAD_DIM=head_dim,\n            eps=eps,\n            HAS_KV=has_kv,\n            BY_HEAD=True,\n            BLOCK=BLOCK,\n        )\n        return\n\n    _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_kernel[(M,)](\n        q,\n        k if has_kv else q,\n        v if has_kv else q,\n        q_weight,\n        k_weight if has_kv else q_weight,\n        q.stride(0),\n        k.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n        v.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n        NUM_Q_HEADS=num_q_heads,\n        NUM_KV_HEADS=num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0,\n        HEAD_DIM=head_dim,\n        eps=eps,\n        HAS_KV=has_kv,\n        BY_HEAD=False,\n        BLOCK=BLOCK,\n    )\n\n\n@triton.jit\ndef _gemma_routing_post_topk_kernel(\n    Logits_ptr,\n    Ids_ptr,\n    Scale_ptr,\n    Out_weights_ptr,\n    Out_ids_ptr,\n    stride_l,\n    stride_ow,\n    stride_oi,\n    K: tl.constexpr,\n    BLOCK_K: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    \"\"\"Fused: softmax(topk_logits) * per_expert_scale[topk_ids] → float32 weights, int32 ids.\n\n    One program per token. K is the number of top-k experts (e.g. 8).\n    \"\"\"\n    row = tl.program_id(0)\n    cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_K)\n    mask = cols < K\n\n    logits = tl.load(\n        Logits_ptr + row * stride_l + cols, mask=mask, other=float(\"-inf\")\n    ).to(tl.float32)\n    ids_i64 = tl.load(Ids_ptr + row * stride_l + cols, mask=mask, other=0)\n\n    # Stable softmax\n    max_val = tl.max(logits, axis=0)\n    exp_val = tl.exp(logits - max_val)\n    sum_exp = tl.sum(exp_val, axis=0)\n    weights = exp_val / sum_exp\n\n    # Gather per_expert_scale and multiply\n    scale = tl.load(Scale_ptr + ids_i64, mask=mask, other=1.0).to(tl.float32)\n    weights = weights * scale\n\n    tl.store(Out_weights_ptr + row * stride_ow + cols, weights, mask=mask)\n    tl.store(Out_ids_ptr + row * stride_oi + cols, ids_i64.to(tl.int32), mask=mask)\n\n\ndef gemma_routing_post_topk(\n    topk_logits: torch.Tensor,\n    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,\n    per_expert_scale: torch.Tensor,\n) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Fused softmax + scale-gather + casts for Gemma4 routing.\n\n    Replaces: softmax(topk_logits) * per_expert_scale[topk_ids] → (f32, i32).\n    \"\"\"\n    B, K = topk_logits.shape\n    BLOCK_K = triton.next_power_of_2(K)\n    out_weights = torch.empty((B, K), dtype=torch.float32, device=topk_logits.device)\n    out_ids = torch.empty((B, K), dtype=torch.int32, device=topk_logits.device)\n\n    _gemma_routing_post_topk_kernel[(B,)](\n        topk_logits,\n        topk_ids,\n        per_expert_scale,\n        out_weights,\n        out_ids,\n        topk_logits.stride(0),\n        out_weights.stride(0),\n        out_ids.stride(0),\n        K=K,\n        BLOCK_K=BLOCK_K,\n    )\n    return out_weights, out_ids\n\n\ndef gemma_dual_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n    x1: torch.Tensor,\n    weight1: torch.Tensor,\n    x2: torch.Tensor,\n    weight2: torch.Tensor,\n    weight3: torch.Tensor,\n    residual: torch.Tensor,\n    scalar: torch.Tensor,\n    eps1: float = 1e-6,\n    eps2: float = 1e-6,\n    eps3: float = 1e-6,\n) -> torch.Tensor:\n    \"\"\"Fused (rmsnorm(rmsnorm(x1,w1) + rmsnorm(x2,w2), w3) + residual) * scalar.\"\"\"\n    assert x1.dim() == 2 and x1.stride(-1) == 1\n    M, N = x1.shape\n    BLOCK_SIZE = triton.next_power_of_2(N)\n    out = torch.empty_like(x1)\n\n    _gemma_dual_rmsnorm_residual_kernel[(M,)](\n        x1,\n        weight1,\n        x2,\n        weight2,\n        weight3,\n        residual,\n        scalar,\n        out,\n        x1.stride(0),\n        x2.stride(0),\n        residual.stride(0),\n        out.stride(0),\n        N,\n        eps1,\n        eps2,\n        eps3,\n        BLOCK_SIZE=BLOCK_SIZE,\n    )\n    return out\n\n\n@triton.jit\ndef _gemma4_routing_kernel(\n    gating_ptr,  # [T, E] router logits, any float dtype\n    per_expert_scale_ptr,  # [E] per-expert scale (any float dtype)\n    topk_weights_ptr,  # [T, K] fp32 out\n    topk_ids_ptr,  # [T, K] int32 out\n    stride_g_t,  # stride of gating in the token dim\n    E: tl.constexpr,\n    K: tl.constexpr,\n    BLOCK_E: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    pid = tl.program_id(0)\n    offs_e = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_E)\n    valid = offs_e < E\n\n    logits = tl.load(\n        gating_ptr + pid * stride_g_t + offs_e,\n        mask=valid,\n        other=-float(\"inf\"),\n    ).to(tl.float32)\n\n    # Pack (sort_key, expert_id) into one int64 so a single signed-ascending\n    # tl.sort yields logits in descending float order. The key bijection is\n    # anti-monotone on the float value, and the <<32 shift moves its high bit\n    # into the int64 sign bit. Ties break by expert id ascending. Invalid\n    # lanes use a max key so they sort last.\n    MIN32 = -2147483648\n    logit_bits = logits.to(tl.int32, bitcast=True)\n    sign = logit_bits >> 31\n    key = tl.where(sign == 0, logit_bits ^ -1, logit_bits ^ MIN32)\n    key = tl.where(valid, key, 0x7FFFFFFF)\n    sk64 = key.to(tl.int64) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF\n    packed = (sk64 << 32) | offs_e.to(tl.int64)\n\n    sorted_p = tl.sort(packed, descending=False)\n    all_keys = ((sorted_p >> 32) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n    all_ids = (sorted_p & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n\n    # Invert the key bijection to recover the original logit value.\n    sign_k = all_keys >> 31\n    all_bits = tl.where(sign_k < 0, all_keys ^ -1, all_keys ^ MIN32)\n    all_logits = all_bits.to(tl.float32, bitcast=True)\n\n    # softmax over the top-K logits; max sits at index 0 (sorted descending).\n    top_mask = offs_e < K\n    max_l = tl.max(tl.where(top_mask, all_logits, -float(\"inf\")), axis=0)\n    raw_exp = tl.where(top_mask, tl.exp(all_logits - max_l), 0.0)\n\n    denom = tl.sum(raw_exp, axis=0)\n    denom = tl.where(denom > 0.0, denom, 1.0)\n    weights = raw_exp / denom\n\n    scales = tl.load(\n        per_expert_scale_ptr + all_ids.to(tl.int64),\n        mask=top_mask,\n        other=1.0,\n    ).to(tl.float32)\n    weights = weights * scales\n\n    base_off = pid * K + offs_e\n    tl.store(topk_weights_ptr + base_off, weights, mask=top_mask)\n    tl.store(topk_ids_ptr + base_off, all_ids, mask=top_mask)\n\n\ndef gemma4_fused_routing(\n    gating_output: torch.Tensor,\n    per_expert_scale: torch.Tensor,\n    topk: int,\n) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"One-launch Gemma4 router.\n\n    Args:\n        gating_output: [T, E] router logits in any floating dtype; will be\n            cast to fp32 inside the kernel.\n        per_expert_scale: [E] per-expert scale, any floating dtype.\n        topk: number of experts to keep per token.\n\n    Returns:\n The key bijection is\n    # anti-monotone on the float value, and the <<32 shift moves its high bit\n    # into the int64 sign bit. Ties break by expert id ascending. Invalid\n    # lanes use a max key so they sort last.\n    MIN32 = -2147483648\n    logit_bits = logits.to(tl.int32, bitcast=True)\n    sign = logit_bits >> 31\n    key = tl.where(sign == 0, logit_bits ^ -1, logit_bits ^ MIN32)\n    key = tl.where(valid, key, 0x7FFFFFFF)\n    sk64 = key.to(tl.int64) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF\n    packed = (sk64 << 32) | offs_e.to(tl.int64)\n\n    sorted_p = tl.sort(packed, descending=False)\n    all_keys = ((sorted_p >> 32) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n    all_ids = (sorted_p & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n\n    # Invert the key bijection to recover the original logit value.\n    sign_k = all_keys >> 31\n    all_bits = tl.where(sign_k < 0, all_keys ^ -1, all_keys ^ MIN32)\n    all_logits = all_bits.to(tl.float32, bitcast=True)\n\n    # softmax over the top-K logits; max sits at index 0 (sorted descending).\n    top_mask = offs_e < K\n    max_l = tl.max(tl.where(top_mask, all_logits, -float(\"inf\")), axis=0)\n    raw_exp = tl.where(top_mask, tl.exp(all_logits - max_l), 0.0)\n\n    denom = tl.sum(raw_exp, axis=0)\n    denom = tl.where(denom > 0.0, denom, 1.0)\n    weights = raw_exp / denom\n\n    scales = tl.load(\n        per_expert_scale_ptr + all_ids.to(tl.int64),\n        mask=top_mask,\n        other=1.0,\n    ).to(tl.float32)\n    weights = weights * scales\n\n    base_off = pid * K + offs_e\n    tl.store(topk_weights_ptr + base_off, weights, mask=top_mask)\n    tl.store(topk_ids_ptr + base_off, all_ids, mask=top_mask)","new_string":"@triton.jit\ndef _gemma4_routing_kernel(\n    gating_ptr,  # [T, E] router logits, any float dtype\n    per_expert_scale_ptr,  # [E] per-expert scale (any float dtype)\n    topk_weights_ptr,  # [T, K] fp32 out\n    topk_ids_ptr,  # [T, K] int32 out\n    stride_g_t,  # stride of gating in the token dim\n    E: tl.constexpr,\n    K: tl.constexpr,\n    BLOCK_E: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    pid = tl.program_id(0)\n    offs_e = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_E)\n    valid = offs_e < E\n\n    logits = tl.load(\n        gating_ptr + pid * stride_g_t + offs_e,\n        mask=valid,\n        other=-float(\"inf\"),\n    ).to(tl.float32)\n\n    # Pack (sort_key, expert_id) into one int64 so a single signed-ascending\n    # tl.sort yields logits in descending float order.
